rationale: Ground truth centers on three real reviewer concerns: the design debate over reading cudagraph_mode in modeling vs. the runner (gcanlin/R2-Y), the TODO documenting that mtp seed reproducibility breaks under full cudagraphs with decode batch>1, and the NPU yaml fix needed for compilation_config (Wallbreazzz). Y explicitly engages all three — validating the TODO's accuracy, flagging the silent seeding regression and requesting an accuracy A/B, and questioning the NPU stage-1 PIECEWISE addition — plus catches two genuinely stale/misleading yaml comments and the duplicate omni_pooler_payload_include_hidden assignment (also caught by X). X's four 'major' findings are all speculative test-coverage requests never raised by any human reviewer, entirely missing the NPU yaml hunk and only glancingly touching the design/seed concerns in an unelaborated sweep bullet, so recall is much lower despite plausible-looking grep evidence. Both are reasonably actionable with concrete file/line pointers, but Y's grounding in the actual diff content (comment-vs-value contradictions) is more clearly verifiable than X's untested claims about test coverage.
